issues.description: The diff removes `await` from `run_verification(worktree, workflow.config)`, but `run_verification` in orchestrator/src/orchestrator/verify.py:188 is still defined as `async def run_verification(...)` and every other call site in verify.py (lines 289, 296, 333, 344, 347) still awaits it. As a result, `verify` is now a coroutine object, not a `VerifyResult`. The very next access, `verify.passed` (line 112), will raise `AttributeError: 'coroutine' object has no attribute 'passed'` at runtime, and Python will emit a `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'run_verification' was never awaited`. The added log statement (line ~114) also dereferences `.passed`, `.lint_output`, `.type_output` on the coroutine and will fail first. Because `collect_metrics` is wrapped in a try/except in runner.py:244–246, eval runs will silently lose all tests/lint/types/verification signal in metrics — exactly the opposite of the stated intent of the logging addition.
issues.suggested_fix: Restore the `await`: `verify = await run_verification(worktree, workflow.config)`. If the goal was to drop the async, first convert `run_verification` (and all its internal `await _run_cmd(...)` / `asyncio.gather` usages) to sync, and update all five other call sites — but that is a much larger refactor and almost certainly not desired for a 'collect metrics' path that already runs in an async workflow.

issues.description: The log line re-derives `not verify.lint_output` and `not verify.type_output`, duplicating the exact expressions used two lines later to populate `m.lint_clean` / `m.typecheck_clean`. This invites drift if those semantics change.
issues.suggested_fix: Move the log statement to after `m = EvalMetrics(...)` and log the already-computed fields: `logger.info('Metrics for task %s: tests=%s lint=%s types=%s plan=%.1f%%', task.get('id', '?'), m.tests_pass, m.lint_clean, m.typecheck_clean, m.plan_completion_pct * 100)`. That also naturally sidesteps the dead `if verify else 'N/A'` branch (see next issue).

issues.description: The `verify.passed if verify else 'N/A'` ternary has an unreachable else branch: `run_verification` in verify.py always returns a `VerifyResult` (it never returns `None`), so `verify` is always truthy. This mirrors an existing pattern on lines 112–114 that is also effectively dead, but the new logging adds a third copy. It's misleading to readers because it implies verification can legitimately be missing.
issues.suggested_fix: Drop the `if verify else ...` guard entirely once the `await` is restored, since the postcondition of `run_verification` guarantees a `VerifyResult`. Apply this to the existing lines 112–114 at the same time (optional cleanup).

issues.description: Adding `# noqa: C901` silences the cyclomatic-complexity lint without addressing it. The function is already mixing: plan parsing, verification re-run, review aggregation, git stats, throughput computation, and construction. Suppressing the warning in the same commit that adds more inline logging nudges the function further over the threshold.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract cohesive helpers (e.g. `_compute_plan_completion(workflow)`, `_compute_throughput(wf_metrics, config)`) so the noqa is unnecessary. At minimum, include a short comment explaining why the suppression is tolerated for now and link to a follow-up task.

issues.description: There are zero tests that exercise `collect_metrics` (grep of the orchestrator tree finds it only in metrics.py and runner.py). That is precisely why the await-removal bug above is not caught by the test suite: a single test that monkeypatches `run_verification` to return a fake `VerifyResult` and asserts the resulting `EvalMetrics.tests_pass` / `lint_clean` / `typecheck_clean` would have failed immediately when the `await` was dropped. df_task_12 already tracks adding tests for `verify.py`; a companion test for `collect_metrics` is appropriate.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `orchestrator/tests/test_metrics.py` with at least: (1) happy path with stubbed workflow, artifacts, and `run_verification` returning a passing `VerifyResult`; (2) failing verification produces `tests_pass=False` and correct `lint_clean` / `typecheck_clean`; (3) empty plan yields `plan_completion=0.0` without divide-by-zero. Patch `orchestrator.verify.run_verification` via `monkeypatch` to avoid running real subprocesses.

issues.description: `collect_metrics` re-invokes `run_verification` on the worktree after the workflow has already completed its own verification passes. This is an extra full run of tests + lint + typecheck per evaluated task (already true before this diff, but the newly added log makes it more visible). On slow suites this doubles wall-clock eval time and can mask flakes as real regressions. Not introduced by this diff, so non-blocking — but worth a TODO.
issues.suggested_fix: If the workflow already stores its last `VerifyResult` on `workflow.artifacts` or `workflow.metrics`, read it from there instead of re-running. Otherwise, add a cached `_last_verify` attribute on `TaskWorkflow` that `collect_metrics` can prefer.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This diff contains a blocking async-correctness bug: it removes `await` from the call to `run_verification` on metrics.py:94, but that function is still `async def` in verify.py (line 188), and every other call site in verify.py still awaits it. `verify` becomes a coroutine object, so the immediately following `verify.passed`, `verify.lint_output`, `verify.type_output` accesses — including those in the newly added log statement — will raise `AttributeError` at runtime. Because `collect_metrics` is wrapped in a broad try/except in runner.py:244–246, this will fail silently and every eval run will lose its tests/lint/types signal — exactly the opposite of the diff's stated goal of improving eval-failure debuggability. Restore the `await` (or, if a sync conversion was intended, convert `run_verification` and update all five other call sites). Separately, the new log re-derives `not verify.lint_output` / `not verify.type_output` which duplicates logic used two lines later when constructing `EvalMetrics` — logging the already-built `m` instead would be cleaner. The `# noqa: C901` just silences the complexity lint; the underlying function mixes plan/verify/review/git/throughput concerns and would benefit from extraction. Finally, `collect_metrics` has no unit tests anywhere in the orchestrator tree — a single test with a stubbed `run_verification` would have caught the blocker immediately.
